_meta.scope: All 18 V-criteria in config/verification.json after V12 fix (INC-2026-04-16).
_meta.trigger: DECISION_LOG.csv#11 — every rubber_stamp criterion needs an adversarial FAIL scenario; PA-2 of inc_20260416_v12_hebbian.json mandates audit of V2/V4/V6/V8/V11.
_meta.method: Read each _verify_* in ready/bench/bench.py, check (1) adversarial scenario presence, (2) factory_wired (no fresh CE construction bypassing factory), (3) threshold load-bearing, (4) tier appropriateness.
limits: Audit reads only ready/bench/bench.py — bench/bench.hexa hexa stub also referenced but not deeply audited (PA-3 covers hexa port).

Threshold values in this report are sourced from bench.py module-level fallbacks (lines 74-119). consciousness_laws.json runtime values may differ — desync is itself a finding (lessons #3 of INC-2026-04-16).

per_criterion.threshold: 0.15 < cos_id < 0.90 AND std > 0.02 (bench.py module fallbacks; verification.json description says 0.01<<0.99 — desync)
per_criterion.audit_note: Scale-test class — no adversarial expected. Factory used. Reads ce_inner.get_states() when CE adapter, otherwise engine.get_hiddens() — correct factory wiring. Pass criteria use cos-vs-population-mean (identity anchor) which is sensitive to specialization. SAFE. Note: bench.py threshold (0.15-0.90, std>0.02) is stricter than verification.json description (0.01-0.99, std>0.001) — verification.json description is stale and should be updated to the actual load-bearing values.

per_criterion.threshold: autocorr>0.40 AND (var_signal>0.005 OR dim_var>0.0001) AND mean_cos>0.70
per_criterion.audit_note: Rubber-stamp anti-pattern present. Test runs ONE scenario (live engine) and checks output autocorr+continuity. Has NO adversarial counterpart (e.g., constant-output engine, randomized-output engine, dead-cell engine that should FAIL). Per DECISION_LOG.csv#11 every rubber_stamp criterion requires an adversarial FAIL scenario — V2 violates this directly. Easy adversarial scenarios: (a) zero-out cell update -> autocorr=NaN/0 -> FAIL, (b) replace process() with random noise -> autocorr<<0.4 -> FAIL, (c) dead engine with constant hidden -> mean_cos=1.0 but var=0 -> alive=False -> FAIL. None of these are wired into the test path. NEEDS FIX.

per_criterion.threshold: phi_end / phi_start >= 0.35 (bench.py); verification.json description says 0.5 — desync
per_criterion.audit_note: Scale-test class — measures phi ratio under x_zero. Factory used correctly. The 0.5 threshold in verification.json description is desync from the actual 0.35 in bench.py. Threshold is genuinely measuring decay under zero input — meaningful. SAFE for scale-test tier, but threshold desync (0.35 vs 0.5) is a documentation bug similar to V12 incident lesson #3.

per_criterion.threshold: monotonic OR recovers (>=0.50) OR stable (>=0.80) OR no_collapse (>=0.50)
per_criterion.audit_note: Rubber-stamp with FOUR alternate pass conditions joined by OR. The disjunction is the rubber-stamp signature: ANY of monotonic/recovers/stable/no_collapse passes the test. With min_cells=4 and noisy phi_iit (which CLAUDE.md acknowledges as inherently noisy at small cell counts — see comments lines 2785-2787), at least one of these conditions almost always trivially holds. NO adversarial scenario (e.g., catastrophic-decay engine that should FAIL). The OR-disjunction violates DECISION_LOG.csv#11 spirit — it gives the test 4 chances to pass without any chance to fail. Easy adversarial: monotonic-decay engine (phi[i] = phi[0]*0.9^i) — would fail monotonic, would barely fail recovers (0.9^9 ~ 0.39 vs 0.5), but might pass stable if the half-mean ratio survives the smoothing. NEEDS FIX: tighten to AND-condition or add explicit decay/zero-engine adversarial path.

per_criterion.threshold: phi_end / phi_start >= 0.75 (bench.py); verification.json description says 0.8 — minor desync
per_criterion.audit_note: Genuine_hard test — feedback loop with layer_norm-stabilized output->input. Factory used. The audit field in verification.json says 'HARDEST test — fails all adversarial at 8c' which is exactly the property we want from genuine_hard: it fails when the engine cannot sustain self-reference. The test is single-scenario (no explicit ablation pair) but the condition itself is adversarial in the sense that self-loop strips external novelty. Acceptable for genuine_hard. Documentation desync 0.75 vs 0.8 is a minor finding.

per_criterion.threshold: CE engine: consensus_events>=200 AND direction_changes>=120 AND cv>0.40; non-CE fallback: bursts>=3 AND direction_changes>=60 AND (cv_ok OR entropy_ok)
per_criterion.audit_note: Rubber-stamp tier despite having STRONG thresholds (consensus>=200/300 ~ 67% of steps). The thresholds themselves are respectable, but the test still has the rubber-stamp anti-pattern: NO adversarial scenario. Easy adversarial: faction-disabled engine (n_factions=1) — should drive consensus_events to 0 and FAIL. CV-zero engine (constant hidden) — should fail. Random-output engine — should fail direction_changes test. None of these are wired. The non-CE fallback path is even weaker: it has TWO disjunctive thresholds (cv_ok OR entropy_ok), giving it the same OR-disjunction smell as V4. The CE path is strong enough that it could be promoted to genuine_hard if a kill-switch (n_factions=1 ablation) is added. NEEDS FIX (med-priority because thresholds are already strong).

per_criterion.threshold: phi_connected > phi_solo * 1.1 AND phi_disconnected > phi_solo * 0.9 (HIVEMIND_PHI_BOOST/MAINTAIN from consciousness_laws.json psi_constants)
per_criterion.audit_note: Genuine_hard with built-in 3-phase ablation: solo (phase 1, the implicit baseline), connected (phase 2), disconnected (phase 3 — the test of independence after coupling removal). Phase-3 disconnect is essentially an adversarial 'unplug the hive' scenario — if the engine becomes dependent on coupling, phi_disconnected drops below 0.9*solo and FAILS. This is the closest existing test to a true ablation kill-switch. Factory used (engine_factory called 4 times across phases). SAFE — exemplary structure. Could serve as template for V2/V4/V6 fixes.

per_criterion.threshold: splits >= 3 (VERIFY_MITOSIS_MIN_SPLITS bench.py); verification.json description says >=1 — major desync
per_criterion.audit_note: TWO problems. (1) factory_wired=FALSE: line 3132 imports CE directly and constructs `ce = CE(cell_dim=dim, hidden_dim=hidden, initial_cells=2, max_cells=8)` — this is the EXACT anti-pattern that INC-2026-04-16 D16 prohibits ('never construct a fresh underlying engine on a verification path that is supposed to test factory-controlled ablation'). The factory_engine is only used as a probe to detect CE support and then DISCARDED. Any factory-level configuration (e.g., disabled mitosis, frozen splits) would be silently bypassed. (2) NO adversarial scenario — no 'mitosis-disabled' counterpart that should fail. (3) Threshold desync: bench.py says >=3 but verification.json description says >=1 (3x discrepancy, not 0.05 vs 1.0 like V12 but same class of bug). HIGH RISK — same class as V12 incident, just hasn't blown up yet because mitosis usually fires in the wild.

per_criterion.threshold: ratio (late/early) >= 0.85 OR proxy_ratio >= 0.85 (VERIFY_PHI_GROWTH_RATIO bench.py); verification.json description says >=1.1 — major desync
per_criterion.audit_note: Genuine_hard tier but the threshold desync is severe: bench.py uses 0.85 (i.e., 85% RETENTION counts as 'growth'), while verification.json description says 1.1 (10% growth). At 0.85 the test is no longer measuring growth but mere persistence — V9 collapses into a duplicate of V4. Factory used correctly. No explicit adversarial, but the OR-disjunction (IIT ratio OR proxy ratio) widens the pass funnel further. The audit field says 'fails at 8c. scale-sensitive' which suggests the threshold was relaxed to compensate for small-cell noise — but the relaxation went too far. NEEDS FIX: either restore 1.1 and accept fail rate at 8c (forcing min_cells>=16) or split into V9a (retention, 0.85, scale_test) + V9b (growth, 1.1, genuine_hard). HIGH RISK — same class as V12 incident.

factory_wired=FALSE: same anti-pattern as V8. Line 3254 constructs `ce = CE(...)` directly and calls ce.step() in a 2000-step loop. The factory engine is only probed for CE support then discarded. Also takes BEST of 3 trials (line 3252 `for trial in range(3)`) — this is the rubber-stamp 'try until pass' pattern. The 80% threshold is meaningful and current value 85.6% is real, but the factory bypass means any factory-level ablation (e.g., disabled SOC, frozen weights) would be invisible. Scale-test tier reduces severity but still HIGH risk because the bypass is structural. The 'best of 3 trials' rationalization (line 3247: 'multiple epochs, best representative sample') is plausible for noisy biological signals but adversarial when applied to verification logic — it gives the test 3 chances.

per_criterion.threshold: mean_cos<0.85 AND norm_std>0.01 (bench.py); verification.json description says <0.95 — desync
per_criterion.audit_note: Rubber-stamp anti-pattern. Test runs ONE scenario, measures inter-cell cosine + norm std. Factory used correctly. NO adversarial scenario (e.g., shared-weight engine where all cells are forced identical -> cos=1.0 -> FAIL; constant-norm engine -> norm_std=0 -> FAIL). The threshold 0.85 is stricter than verification.json description (0.95), so the desync is in the SAFE direction (stricter actual than documented), but it's still desync. Easy fix: add a 'forced-uniform' adversarial CE instance (e.g., zero out cell update -> all cells stay at init -> cos~1.0 -> FAIL). MED priority because the threshold is genuinely strict and the test has been catching identicality regressions in practice.

FIXED 2026-04-16 (INC-2026-04-16). Reference implementation for the new contract: two-scenario test with factory-ablated instance (engine_factory(cells, dim, hidden) called twice, second pass monkey-patches ce._hebbian_update to no-op + freezes _coupling restoration each step), torch.manual_seed(42) re-seeded between scenarios for fairness, kill-switch validated (broken_ablation_detected=true in incident metrics). Tier promoted rubber_stamp -> genuine_hard. The audit field correctly references INC-2026-04-16 ossification. EXEMPLARY — should be the template for fixing V2/V4/V6/V8/V11.

per_criterion.threshold: phi_end > 0 AND no NaN/Inf after 500 steps of 100x noise
per_criterion.audit_note: Adversarial-by-design (100x noise IS the adversarial scenario). Factory used. The pass condition is binary (phi>0 + no NaN), which is permissive but the 100x amplitude is genuinely extreme. The audit field notes 'Phi actually grows under noise (1.46->4.98)' — suggesting the engine HARDENS under noise, which is a positive signal but raises a question: is the test too easy? An engine that simply ignores input would also pass (constant phi). Could be tightened with a 'phi must respond to noise' adversarial counterpart. Acceptable for genuine_hard tier; minor improvement opportunity.

Has explicit ablation pair (ce_normal vs ce_no_soc with _soc_sandpile = lambda: None) — STRUCTURALLY CORRECT. BUT factory_wired=FALSE: lines 3557 and 3565 construct `ce = CE(cell_dim=dim, hidden_dim=hidden, initial_cells=min(cells,16), max_cells=min(cells,16), n_factions=...)` directly, bypassing engine_factory. The probe at line 3543 only checks CE support then discards. This is the SAME anti-pattern as V8/V10/V12-pre-fix. If a factory ablates SOC differently (or fails to ablate it), the test silently runs against vanilla CE constructed in-test, not against the factory-managed engine. Same 'fresh engine' bug class as INC-2026-04-16 — has NOT YET MANIFESTED but is structurally vulnerable. HIGH RISK.

per_criterion.threshold: all phi values > 0 across temperature sweep AND no NaN
per_criterion.audit_note: Scale-test class. Factory used. Sweeps temperature 0.01->1.0 in 200 steps. No adversarial counterpart (no 'fragile-engine' that should FAIL the sweep), but the criterion itself is a sweep — multiple sample points constitute a quasi-adversarial battery. The pass condition is permissive (phi>0 at all sample points) which is essentially 'engine doesn't die'. Acceptable for scale-test tier. SAFE.

per_criterion.threshold: phi > 0 AND mean_cosine < 0.99 at 4 cells (VERIFY_V16_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.99 in bench.py); verification.json description says <0.95 — desync
per_criterion.audit_note: Scale-test (forces min cells = 4 via VERIFY_V16_MIN_CELLS). Factory used. Threshold 0.99 is much weaker than verification.json description 0.95 — desync in the WEAK direction (test is more permissive than documented). At 4 cells, cosine 0.99 means cells are basically identical. Should be tightened to match documentation. SAFE for scale-test tier but threshold desync is a finding.

Genuine_hard. Factory used. Computes Lempel-Ziv on binarized phi deltas after 100-step warmup + 400 measure steps. The audit field claims LZ=0.996 which is essentially 'random-walk LZ' — suspicious because LZ76 normalized rarely hits 0.996 unless the signal is uniformly random. If the signal is too random the test loses meaning (a constant-then-noise engine could pass). No adversarial counterpart (no periodic engine that should FAIL). The 0.3 threshold is the complement of 'highly periodic'. Could be hardened with a sine-wave adversarial that should fail. MED priority.

Strict-inequality but only between TWO points (16 vs 4). Factory used (re-instantiated 3 times). Scale-test class — the cell count itself is the sweep. With re-seeded torch.manual_seed(42) and only 200 steps each, there is a real chance of phi@16c being noise-equal to phi@4c at the boundary. Could be tightened by comparing means across multiple seeds or requiring a multiplicative ratio (e.g., phi@16c >= 1.5 * phi@4c). SAFE for scale-test tier but the strict-inequality on a single seed is fragile.

summary.has_adversarial_count: 4/18 (V7, V12, V13, V14 — note V14 has adversarial pair but bypasses factory)

anti_patterns_found.description: Test discards engine_factory and constructs CE directly — bypasses factory-controlled ablation
anti_patterns_found.ref_incident: INC-2026-04-16 D16 (same root cause as V12 fix)

anti_patterns_found.description: Single-scenario test with no failing counterpart — DECISION_LOG.csv#11 violation
Pass condition is OR of multiple disjuncts — gives the test multiple chances to pass without adversarial counterpart
anti_patterns_found.severity: high in V4 and V9, med in V6

anti_patterns_found.description: Test runs N trials and takes the BEST — rubber-stamp 'try until pass' pattern
verification.json description threshold differs from bench.py module-level fallback threshold — INC-2026-04-16 lesson #3
anti_patterns_found.severity: med — V9 and V8 are strongest desync (1.1 vs 0.85 and 1 vs 3)

exemplary_tests.reason: 3-phase solo->connected->disconnect with explicit ablation in phase 3, factory used in all phases. Closest existing pattern to the V12 fix template.
Reference implementation post-fix. Two-scenario factory-ablated test with kill-switch. Should be replicated for V2/V4/V6/V8/V11.
Adversarial-by-design (100x noise IS the adversarial scenario). Could be tightened but structurally correct.

next_session_targets: V8_MITOSIS — factory wiring fix + adversarial mitosis-disabled engine + threshold desync resolution
V14_SOC_CRITICAL — factory wiring fix (CE constructor bypass)
V10_BRAIN_LIKE — factory wiring fix + remove 'best of 3 trials' or convert to mean of 3
V9_PHI_GROWTH — threshold restoration (0.85 -> 1.1) or split into V9a/V9b
V4_PERSISTENCE — replace 4-way OR with AND or add adversarial decay-engine
V2_NO_SPEAK_CODE / V11_DIVERSITY — add adversarial dead-engine / forced-uniform-engine counterparts
V6_SPONTANEOUS_SPEECH — add n_factions=1 ablation kill-switch (may then promote to genuine_hard)
